evals.name: display-qualifying-patterns
evals.prompt: Run sw:skill-gen. The project has a skill-signals.json with 3 signals: 'error-handling' (4 increments, confidence 0.8), 'api-patterns' (3 increments, confidence 0.6), and 'naming-conventions' (1 increment, confidence 0.33). MinSignalCount is 3.
evals.expected_output: Display the 2 qualifying patterns (error-handling and api-patterns) with their details. Do not display naming-conventions (below threshold).

assertions.text: Displays error-handling pattern with 4 increments and confidence 0.8
Displays api-patterns with 3 increments and confidence 0.6
Does NOT display naming-conventions (only 1 increment, below minSignalCount of 3)

evals.name: no-signals-available
evals.prompt: Run sw:skill-gen. The project has a skill-signals.json with 2 signals, both with only 1 increment ID each. MinSignalCount is 3.
evals.expected_output: Inform the user that no qualifying patterns were found and explain the minimum threshold.

assertions.text: Outputs a message indicating no qualifying patterns were found
Does NOT attempt to invoke skill-creator or generate any SKILL.md files

evals.name: missing-signals-file
evals.prompt: Run sw:skill-gen. The project does not have a .specweave/state/skill-signals.json file.
evals.expected_output: Inform the user that no signals have been detected yet and suggest running increments with living docs enabled.

assertions.text: Outputs a message indicating no signals detected yet
Mentions running increments with living docs as the way to generate signals
